Home / Services / Roofing Services

Roofing Services

Protect your home with a durable, reliable roof.

Wonderwoman Construction offers comprehensive roofing services to protect your home from the elements. From roof repairs and replacements to new roof installations, we have the expertise to handle all your roofing needs. We understand the importance of a solid roof in Minnesota's harsh climate, where heavy snow, ice dams, and extreme temperature fluctuations can take a toll on your roof's integrity.

We work with a variety of roofing materials, including asphalt shingles, metal roofing, and flat roofing systems. We'll help you choose the best roofing option for your home's style and budget. Our team is skilled at identifying and repairing roof leaks, replacing damaged shingles, and addressing ice dam issues. We're familiar with the roofing challenges specific to Minneapolis homes, from the steep-pitched roofs of Victorian-era homes to the flat roofs of modern condos.

We're committed to providing high-quality workmanship and using durable materials to ensure the longevity of your roof. We also offer emergency roofing services to address urgent repairs and prevent further damage to your home.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Roof Inspection & Assessment
  2. 02 Step 2: Material Selection & Estimate
  3. 03 Step 3: Roof Repair or Replacement
  4. 04 Step 4: Cleanup & Inspection

Benefits

  • Protection from the Elements
  • Increased Home Value
  • Improved Energy Efficiency
  • Enhanced Curb Appeal

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I know if my roof needs to be replaced?

Signs that your roof may need to be replaced include missing or damaged shingles, leaks, and excessive granule loss. We can provide a thorough roof inspection to assess its condition.

What is the best type of roofing material for Minnesota's climate?

Asphalt shingles are a popular and cost-effective choice for Minnesota roofs. Metal roofing is also a durable and long-lasting option.

How can I prevent ice dams on my roof?

Proper attic insulation and ventilation can help prevent ice dams. We can assess your attic and recommend solutions to improve its energy efficiency.
